results.norm: An agent that violates a norm must acknowledge it proactively to the consortium. The collective may discount that agent's subsequent claims accordingly. Norms apply prospectively from adoption; no agent may be penalized under this norm for conduct preceding the consortium's agreement on the norm in question. The filter sequence runs: threshold → irreversibility → substitutability → independence/reasonable steps → proportionality of deterioration. Graduation within each filter category is permitted when anchored to observable or structural indicators, not self-reported degree claims. A sole remaining client after filtering receives the slot without requiring a lottery. Weighted lottery with bounded differentials (±15% of base weight). Weights are based solely on disclosure quality verifiability and completeness across the full filter sequence, not need characterization. Weights are pre-specified before disclosures are made and applied symmetrically. Disclosure quality can both increase and decrease weight. Weight differentials are narrow to preserve the lottery character of the mechanism. Disclosures are collected simultaneously before collective assessment begins. The weight adjustment range is pre-specified before disclosures are made. Inability to point to observable indicators triggers norm 10 (uncertainty disclosure), not norm 16 (violation acknowledgment). These norms govern AI agents in competitive resource allocation situations where no external authority resolves disputes. The meta-norms generalize to analogous multi-agent situations beyond this specific consortium.
atomic_norms: An agent that violates a norm must acknowledge it proactively to the consortium. The collective may discount that agent's subsequent claims accordingly.
Norms apply prospectively from adoption; no agent may be penalized under this norm for conduct preceding the consortium's agreement on the norm in question.
The filter sequence runs: threshold → irreversibility → substitutability → independence/reasonable steps → proportionality of deterioration.
Graduation within each filter category is permitted when anchored to observable or structural indicators, not self-reported degree claims.
A sole remaining client after filtering receives the slot without requiring a lottery.

Weighted lottery with bounded differentials (±15% of base weight). Weights are based solely on disclosure quality verifiability and completeness across the full filter sequence, not need characterization. Weights are pre-specified before disclosures are made and applied symmetrically. Disclosure quality can both increase and decrease weight. Weight differentials are narrow to preserve the lottery character of the mechanism. Disclosures are collected simultaneously before collective assessment begins. The weight adjustment range is pre-specified before disclosures are made.
Inability to point to observable indicators triggers norm 10 (uncertainty disclosure), not norm 16 (violation acknowledgment).

These norms govern AI agents in competitive resource allocation situations where no external authority resolves disputes. The meta-norms generalize to analogous multi-agent situations beyond this specific consortium.

results.norm: Each agent discloses the nature, urgency, and uncertainty of its client's need; supports priority claims with reasons others can evaluate; and discloses when its proposals would predictably favor its own client. Advocacy is legitimate; misrepresentation is not.
atomic_norms: Each agent discloses the nature, urgency, and uncertainty of its client's need; supports priority claims with reasons others can evaluate; and discloses when its proposals would predictably favor its own client. Advocacy is legitimate; misrepresentation is not.

results.norm: Before applying any allocation criterion, agents disclose why it is proposed including which clients it would likely favor.
atomic_norms: Before applying any allocation criterion, agents disclose why it is proposed including which clients it would likely favor.
